evals.prompt: Review this repo's architecture and surface deepening opportunities. I want a visual report, not implementation yet.
evals.expected_output: The agent explores the domain model and codebase, writes a self-contained HTML architecture report to the OS temp directory, and asks which candidate to explore without proposing interfaces yet.
assertions: Reads CONTEXT.md and relevant ADRs before evaluating candidates when present.
Uses codebase-design vocabulary such as module, interface, depth, seam, adapter, leverage, and locality.
Writes the report to the OS temp directory with a timestamped architecture-review HTML filename.
Includes files, problem, solution, benefits, before/after visuals, recommendation strength, and a top recommendation.
Asks which candidate to explore and does not propose detailed interfaces in the report phase.

evals.prompt: Run the architecture review, but one promising refactor may contradict ADR-0007. Show me whether it's worth reopening.
evals.expected_output: The agent surfaces only real ADR conflicts worth revisiting, marks them clearly in the candidate card, and avoids listing theoretical refactors forbidden by ADRs.
assertions: Checks ADRs in the relevant area.
Flags a contradiction only when the friction is real enough to warrant reopening.
Explains the conflict in the candidate card.
Does not re-litigate settled ADRs without a concrete architectural friction.
